data.title: Grieg Seafood ASA: Integrated Annual Report 2025
data.description:
Grieg Seafood ASA publishes its Integrated Annual Report 2025, including sustainability reporting and annual accounts with notes.

Profit before tax from continuing operations totaled NOK 76 million for 2025, unchanged from the Q4 2025 reporting. The Group's net profit for the year from continuing operations is NOK 94 million, the increase is caused by a change in income tax of NOK 45 million compared to the Q4 2025 reporting, where only NOK 49 million were reported for the year 2025. The Group's total comprehensive income for the year is NOK 768 million, also increased by the change in income tax of NOK 45 million from the Q4 reporting, where NOK 723 million were reported for the year 2025. The NOK 45 million change in the net profit for the year 2025 resulted in a positive change in total equity, which increased from NOK 4,457 million to NOK 4,502 million.

The Integrated Annual Report 2025 is published today, available in European Single Electronic Format (ESEF) on the Company's websitehttps://investor.griegseafood.com/reports-&-presentations#annually-and-quarterly

The remuneration report for 2025 is also available as pdf on the Company's websitehttps://investor.griegseafood.com/reports-&-presentations#annually-and-quarterly

For further enquiries, please contact:

Nina Grieg, CEO

Cell phone: +47 953 39 161

Magnus Johannesen, CFO

Cell phone +47 959 13 243

This information is publicly disclosed in accordance with section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act as well as the Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16 April 2014 on market abuse (market abuse regulation). The stock exchange announcement was published by May Hansen, Executive Assistant, Grieg Seafood ASA, on 27 March 2026 at 6:00 a.m. CET.

About Grieg Seafood

Grieg Seafood ASA is one of the world’s leading salmon farmers. Our farms are located in Rogaland in Norway with our headquarter in Bergen, Norway. Grieg Seafood ASA was listed at the Oslo Stock Exchange in June 2007. Sustainable farming practices are the foundation of Grieg Seafood's operations. The lowest possible environmental impact and the best possible fish welfare is both an ethical responsibility and drive economic profitability.

To learn more, please visitwww.griegseafood.com

Attachments

2025 GSF Annual Report

data.title: SwissBorg (BORG) - Vote - 26-30 March 2026
data.description:
SwissBorg is holding a community vote on whether to introduce boosted referral rewards linked to users’ Loyalty Rank. According to the official vote announcement, the proposal would adjust the referral program so that higher-ranked users earn increased rewards for bringing in new users. Mechanically, this redirects more reward emissions toward loyal or heavily invested users, and may incentivize additional BORG holdings or activity if Loyalty Rank depends on staking, balances, or app usage. If passed, this could accelerate user growth and ecosystem volume, but higher reward payouts might also increase token distribution and potential sell pressure from reward farmers.

SwissBorg
@swissborg

New Guardians of the BORG vote is open. ️

Your last opportunity this quarter to shape the future and earn your share of governance rewards.

Remember to :

Lock your $BORG

️ Cast your vote

Earn Guardians rewards - delivered in April

️ Closes 30th March at 23:59 CEST… pic.twitter.com/zBVavygGSG

Mar 26, 2026

data.title: ONFA (OFT) - ONFA Insurance Talk - 27 March 2026
data.description:
ONFA will host an "ONFA Insurance - Securing Digital Assets in an Uncertain World" session on 27 March 2026 at 13:00 UTC, as outlined in the official event teaser. Mechanically, this is a live educational and marketing event rather than an on-chain upgrade, but it is a prime venue for disclosing details on coverage models, risk frameworks, or upcoming insurance products around digital assets. Any new information on how ONFA underwrites risk, sources yield, or protects capital can directly influence perceived protocol safety and revenue potential, which in turn can shift market appetite for holding or staking OFT around and after the session.

ONFA
@onfaofficial

ONFA AMA - Live on X

Topic: “ONFA Insurance - Securing Digital Assets in an Uncertain World”

In a world full of market volatility and uncertainty, how can you truly protect your digital assets?

Join us for an exclusive AMA to discover how ONFA Insurance is shaping a… pic.twitter.com/EWAuyiGExX

Mar 26, 2026

data.title: Aurora (AURORA) - AURORA Alpha Leaks - 27 March 2026
data.description:
Aurora is running an "Alpha Leaks" session on 27 March 2026 featuring Ferran Prat, CEO of NEAR Mobile, streamed live on X and YouTube at 15:00 CET (14:00 UTC), according to the official session announcement. Mechanically, this is a public discussion, but such formats are often used to reveal roadmap items, integrations, or incentive programs between Aurora and NEAR Mobile. Any confirmation of mobile-focused user acquisition, new dApp support, or liquidity incentives could strengthen the narrative around Aurora as a key infrastructure layer in the NEAR ecosystem, driving speculative demand and short-term volatility in AURORA if the information is perceived as materially positive.

Aurora
@auroraisnear

data.title: Factors to watch on March 27
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening higher on Friday, with futures up 0.43%.

* UNILEVER: Unilever and its recently spun-off Magnum ice cream unit were sued for defamation on Thursday by Anuradha Mittal, who was ousted in December as chair of Ben & Jerry's independent board.

* LSEG: Index provider FTSE Russell said on Thursday it will align the minimum free-float requirement for UK and non-UK companies in the FTSE UK Index Series from the June 2026 review.

* JAGUAR LAND ROVER: Jaguar Land Rover has temporarily halted production on some of its car lines at its Solihull plant in Britain, citing a parts issue involving a supplier, the Tata Motors-owned automaker said.

* SMMT: UK vehicle production fell 17.2% year-on-year in February in what the industry called an "extremely worrying" decline, according to data published on Friday.

* CONSUMER SENTIMENT: British consumer sentiment weakened to its lowest in nearly a year in March as households fretted about the potential impact of the Iran war on the economy, as per a survey.

* ECONOMY: Britain's finance minister Rachel Reeves convened the country's six largest banks and building societies on Thursday to assess the impact of the Middle East conflict on households and small businesses.

* OIL: Oil prices fell on Friday and were on track for the steepest weekly decline in six months.

* GOLD: Gold rose over 1% on Friday, buoyed by a weaker dollar and bargain hunting.

* METALS: Copper inched up on Friday and was on track to post its first weekly gain since the U.S.-Israel war on Iran began.

*FTSE: London's FTSE 100 equities index dropped more than 1% on Thursday, as lingering uncertainty over a Middle East ceasefire weighed on sentiment.

* For more on the factors affecting European stocks, please click on:

TODAY'S UK PAPERS

> Financial Times

> Other business headlines

data.title: Key facts: Infosys to buy Optimum Healthcare IT $465M; Analyst upbeat
data.description:
• Infosys to buy Optimum Healthcare IT for up to $465M. Optimum had FY25 revenue $275.9M. Deal closes Q1 FY27 and could add ~1.2% to Infosys FY27 revenue after 9 months.1
  • Analyst Siddharth Khemka of Motilal Oswal Financial Services stated that recent Infosys (INFY) deals have strengthened the company’s outlook, according to his assessment.2

data.title: Key facts: HDFC Bank chair resigns; Sunita Maheshwari reappointed
data.description:
• HDFC Bank chair Atanu Chakraborty resigned after a board dispute over CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan’s tenure; shares fell over 2% on the reports of the disagreement.12
  • HDFC Bank re-appointed Sunita Maheshwari as independent director for March 30, 2026–March 29, 2029, pending shareholder approval; she’s unrelated to current directors and not debarred by authorities.3

data.title: Advent Weighing Expansion Routes for Share-Registry Provider — Interview
data.description:
By Stuart Condie

SYDNEY--Advent International is in talks to expand Australian share-registry provider Automic overseas, with the U.S. private-equity firm weighing options including potential acquisitions.

Boston-based Advent acquired Automic and its cloud-based financial-services platform last year. At the time, it said it would focus on growth in Australia and New Zealand, but that international expansion was also on its agenda.

Talks to expand either organically or through an acquisition are now underway, said Beau Dixon, Managing Director and Head of Australia and New Zealand at Advent.

"Both of those strategies for international expansion are currently under assessment. There are opportunities identified," Dixon said last week.

Dixon did not say where the opportunities were, but said the U.S. was one of several global markets relevant to the Australian operator.

"We think about North America, both U.S. and Canada; we think about the U.K. and we think about other markets in Asia. They all have similar market structures," Dixon said, adding that Advent would continue to assess multiple markets concurrently.

"Expanding organically requires boots on the ground. Expanding through M&A, it's the quality of the team that could execute," Dixon said.

Advent did not disclose terms of the Automic acquisition, which was its first locally since opening its Sydney office in 2024.

A person familiar with the transaction said at the time Automic was valued at 725 million Australian dollars, equivalent to US$499.2 million.

Write to Stuart Condie at stuart.condie@wsj.com

data.title: IOC, HPCL preferred bets; Infosys deals strengthen outlook: Siddharth Khemka
data.description: Valuations have corrected and dividend yields remain supportive, making select oil marketing companies worth tracking despite near-term volatility, Siddharth Khemka of Motilal Oswal Financial Services said. He also expects graphite players to benefit from improving demand in metals and electric vehicles, but governance concerns keep investors cautious in the payments banking space.

data.title: Lenskart valuations leave 'little upside' for shares, HSBC says with target close to post-listing highs
data.description:
Shares of Lenskart Solutions Ltd. recovered from early losses to trade with gains on Friday, March 27.

Brokerage firm HSBC initiated coverage on the newly listed company with a "hold" rating and a price target of ₹513 per share. The price target is close to the stock's post-listing high of ₹543.

HSBC's price target implies an upside potential of 3.6% from Wednesday's closing levels.

Lenskart is backward integrated and the D2C business creates one of the highest-moats in consumer, HSBC said. The total addressable market (TAM) expansion and market share gains offer sustainable growth, it added.

HSBC said Lenskart is seeing build-in margin improvement across both India and international markets.

The brokerage expects Lenskart's revenue and Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation (EBITDA) grow at a Compounded Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 23% and 49% respectively over financial year 2025-2028.

HSBC said that the valuation that Lenskart commands compared to other retailers and platform companies, limits the upside that the stock price can have.

Lenskart shares listed on the stock exchanges at ₹402 per share in October last year.

16 analysts have coverage on Lenskart, of which 12 have a "buy" rating, three say "hold" and one has a "sell" rating.

Shares of Lenskart are trading 0.8% higher on Friday at ₹499.4. The stock is down 7% in the last one month.

data.title: Chinese Conglomerate Citic Reports Higher 2025 Revenue
data.description:
(04:19 GMT) *Citic 2025 Net CNY58.7B Vs. Net CNY58.2B >0267.HK
(04:19 GMT) *Citic 2025 Rev CNY769.26B Vs. CNY747.20B >0267.HK
(04:20 GMT) *Citic 2025 Net Interest Income CNY146.93B Vs. CNY148.37B >0267.HK
(04:21 GMT) *Citic 2025 Sales of Goods, Services CNY478.41B Vs. CNY473.46B >0267.HK
(04:42 GMT) Chinese Conglomerate Citic Reports Higher 2025 Revenue

By Sherry Qin

Chinese conglomerate Citic's 2025 revenue rose slightly as strong performances by its financial services and advanced manufacturing businesses offset the weakness of its consumption and urbanization segments.

The Beijing-based company reported a 3% increase for revenue to 769.26 billion yuan, equivalent to US$111.30 billion. Its net profit edged 0.9% higher to 58.73 billion yuan.

Its financial services segment's revenue rose 6.2% to 290.88 billion yuan, driven by growth in Citic Securities' brokerage business and fee income from Citic Bank. Its advanced materials business recorded a 3% revenue increase to 335.46 billion yuan while its manufacturing segment's revenue rose 12.5%.

The company declared a final dividend of 0.385 yuan per share for 2025, taking its annual dividend to 0.585 yuan per share, up 6.4%.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 10:10 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 1.3% at 74,274, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 1.3% to 23,004, tracking global equities, as fading hopes of a resolution to the Iran war weighed on sentiment.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee fell to a record low of 94.2950 versus the U.S. dollar, down 0.3% on the day, as investors fretted over the economic risks to India from a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 96.7425 rupees, with the yield up 7 bps at 6.9404%, as oil prices remain elevated and as traders reacted to the a cut in fuel excise duties, which is detrimental for government finances.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was flat at 5.92%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose nearly 17 bps to 6.65%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.40%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.5%.

data.title: Tsingtao's Rising Average Selling Price Could Support Margin Expansion — Market Talk
data.description: Tsingtao Brewery's rising average selling price could support robust margin expansion this year, says DBS Group Research analysts in a note. The brewer outperformed the industry's volume decline as it swiftly adapted to new channels and tailored its product launches, the analysts say. Its mid-to-high end beers are likely to continue gaining traction this year, they add, noting they are key drivers of the company's product mix upgrade. Major sporting events should support beer consumption and boost sales volume, they say. DBS maintains its buy rating and H share target price of HK$75.00. Shares are up 0.9% at HK$49.08. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Korea's Incoming Central Bank Chief Faces Tough Balancing Act
data.description:
By Jihye Lee and Fabiana Negrin Ochoa

South Korea's pick to lead its central bank could tilt policy in a more hawkish direction, though what that means for interest rates remains uncertain.

If confirmed as Bank of Korea governor, Shin Hyun-song will inherit a challenging economic backdrop: weak domestic growth, a pressured won and renewed geopolitical tensions that risk triggering an inflation shock as energy prices rise.

The Oxford-trained economist would take over from Rhee Chang-yong next month after 12 years at the Bank for International Settlements and chair his first rate-setting in May.

Markets are pricing in policy tightening as the Middle East war drives up commodity prices. While rate hikes have limited effect on inflation that is boosted by supply shocks, analysts say a prolonged conflict could force central banks' hands.

In a recent BIS media briefing, Shin addressed the policy trade-off.

"Theoretically if it's a supply shock, certainly if it's a temporary one, these are the textbook examples where you should look through and not react with monetary policy," he said, especially if inflation expectations are well-anchored.

"We are in a period when the post-Covid era inflation has left a very deep mark," and the reassessment of how central banks should act is perhaps a reflection of that, he said.

"The fact that market prices have moved means that you have to take those into account when you conduct monetary policy, but some moves are very short term, purely a market event, so central banks need to judge based on fundamentals."

Markets appear to be expecting more aggressive BOK policy action.

Interest-rate swaps imply a quarter-point increase within six months and up to 100 basis points of tightening over the next year, DBS senior economist Ma Tieying said. DBS's economists see that as overdone, citing an uneven recovery, weak domestic demand and sensitivity to external shocks.

"We expect his leadership to lean toward a balanced and pragmatic policy approach rather than outright tightening bias," Ma said.

Still, some economists see scope for pre-emptive tightening if the Middle East crisis worsens.

"Though his views will come into closer view at the parliamentary confirmation hearing, Shin's past remarks, coupled with Korea's macroeconomic conditions, suggest he will take a relatively hawkish policy stance," ING economist Min Joo Kang said.

ING's base case is a July hike, though May isn't ruled out if inflation risks intensify.

"We believe the policy tendency of Hyun Song Shin could be classified as [a] pragmatic hawk," Citigroup economist Jinwook Kim said. Citi expects 25 basis-point hikes both in July and October, bringing the rate to 3.00%.

Regardless of timing, Shin's focus on global liquidity, foreign exchange and systemic risk signals a shift toward more internationally attuned policymaking at the BOK.

"We expect the incoming governor to adopt a more cautious policy stance," said Bank of America Merrill Lynch's Benson Wu, citing Shin's expertise in a volatile, geopolitically driven environment.

Bank of America added that the May policy meeting--Shin's first--alongside updated projections and inaugural remarks, should offer clearer guidance.

Write to Jihye Lee at jihye.lee@wsj.com and Fabiana Negrin Ochoa at fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com

data.title: Australian Dollar Could Continue to Fall vs. U.S. Dollar, Charts Show — Market Talk
data.description: The Australian dollar could continue to fall versus its U.S. counterpart, based on charts, says Quek Ser Leang of UOB's Global Economics & Markets Research. The Australian dollar dropped sharply against the U.S. dollar and broke below the lower end of a rising wedge pattern on Monday, the senior technical strategist notes. Also, the Australian dollar fell and tested the base of the daily Ichimoku cloud near $0.6870 on Thursday. Moreover, weekly moving average convergence divergence indicator has been moving steadily lower over past few weeks. A clear break below $0.6850-$0.6870 support zone could potentially fuel a sharp drop toward $0.6765, the strategist adds. The Australian dollar is 0.3% higher at $0.6904, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)
